name: Varenyky (Filled Dumplings) (Russian)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Evaporated Milk; NOT Condensed Milk
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the flour and salt to form a well in the center.
2. Add butter and milk, mixing lightly until the flour is absorbed. If necessary, add a little warm water.
3. Knead the dough on a floured surface until it sticks together. Cover and let rest for a few minutes.
4. Knead again until smooth. Cover and set aside. The dough should be soft since more flour will be added when rolled out.
5. Alternatively, mix flour, salt, and butter in a processor until a ball forms. Add liquids slowly while the machine runs. Let rest briefly, then process until smooth. Place in a lightly oiled bowl, turn to cover, and let sit for about 30 minutes. It can be wrapped in plastic and refrigerated for up to a day before rolling out.
6. Divide the dough into four parts. Work with one portion on a floured surface.
7. Roll into a circle, starting from the center and rolling outward to maintain even thickness. Turn over and roll again from the center until about 1/8-inch thick.
8. Loosen the bottom with your hand. Cut into rounds using a 3-inch biscuit cutter or wine glass.
9. Place 1 tablespoon of filling on one side of each round, flip the other half over, and seal with fingers or a fork. Ensure proper sealing to prevent filling leakage during cooking.
10. Place dumplings on a floured cookie sheet, covering with a towel. Repeat with remaining dough, saving scraps for last. Use minimal flour to avoid toughness.
11. Gently drop 12 to 15 varenyky into boiling water in a large, wide pot or Dutch oven. Do not overcrowd; stir and cook for about 3 to 4 minutes.
12. Drain dumplings in a colander and place on a lightly oiled cookie sheet, shaking to coat with a thin film to prevent sticking. Do not pile them together as this changes shape.
13. If freezing, remove varenyky when they float to the top (do not overcook). Place on an oiled cookie sheet and freeze until rigid. Store in tightly sealed plastic bags.
14. Serve poached, pan-fried, or steamed with sour cream, chopped sauteed onions, or fried bacon with a little bacon fat.
